name: Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb
name_original: Subida a puerto de Ojén – circular desde Marbella
description: The Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb is a moderate 15.3-mile road cycling route in Sierra Blanca with stunning views and 2075 feet of elevation g
region_intro: Road cycling around Marbella offers a diverse landscape characterized by its unique blend of coastal views, mountainous backdrops, and traditional Andalusian countryside. The region features the Sierra Blanca mountain range, including La Concha Mountain, which provides challenging climbs and panoramic vistas. Inland, cyclists encounter areas like the Istán Lake and olive groves, while the extensive coastline offers flatter routes. This geographical variety creates a wide array of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Marbella The most popular road cycling route is Escape from Marbella to Istán with views of Embalse de la Concepción, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route offers moderate climbs with scenic views of the Istán Lake. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This route leads into the mountains towards Refugio de Juanar, providing significant elevation gain and spectacular views. Local road cyclists also love the Walk from Marbella to San Pedro de Alcántara – circular, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail leading through the coastal promenade, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Road cycling around Marbella is defined by coastal paths, mountain climbs, and routes through traditional white villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal rides to demanding mountain challenges. The routes in Marbella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 6000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Marbella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Marbella?
A: Marbella offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Marbella?
A: Thanks to its favorable microclimate, Marbella is excellent for road cycling almost year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while winter provides mild, sunny conditions perfect for escaping colder climates. Even summer can be enjoyed, especially during early mornings or late afternoons to avoid the peak heat.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Marbella?
A: Yes, Marbella has several easy routes suitable for beginners. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944454533/walk-from-marbella-to-san-pedro-de-alcantara-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walk from Marbella to San Pedro de Alcántara – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the coastal promenade. This route is relatively flat and offers refreshing sea breezes and scenic views.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes heading into the Sierra Blanca mountains offer substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942578625/ojen-refugio-juanar-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route leading towards Refugio de Juanar with spectacular views. For a more demanding experience, consider routes towards Ronda via the A-397, known for its demanding ascents and breathtaking scenery.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on road cycling routes in Marbella?
A: Marbella's routes offer a diverse range of scenic views. You can enjoy refreshing coastal vistas along the Mediterranean, panoramic views of the Sierra Blanca and La Concha Mountain, and picturesque landscapes around Istán Lake. Inland routes also feature traditional Andalusian countryside with olive and cork groves, and charming white villages like Ojén and Benahavís.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges accessible by road bike near Marbella?
A: While most waterfalls and gorges require some off-road exploration, some road cycling routes pass near areas where these natural features can be found. For instance, the Río Verde area, which includes the Hoyo del Bote Waterfall, is part of scenic cycling routes. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Canalón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4498964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Blanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Marbella?
A: The road cycling routes in Marbella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 130 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal paths to challenging mountain climbs, and the beautiful Andalusian scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Marbella?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Marbella serves as an excellent starting point. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10285247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Genal River – Puerto El Madroño loop from Playa de Levante&lt;/a&gt; cover over 87 km with significant elevation, offering a full day's adventure through diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near charming white villages such as Istán, Ojén, and Benahavís, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and sometimes accommodation. The coastal promenade routes also offer numerous amenities. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify these points of interest beforehand.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles to see along the routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical sites. For example, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5820655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marbella Castle&lt;/a&gt; within the town itself. Further afield, highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/679625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sohail Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible in the broader region, often incorporated into longer rides.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944454533/walk-from-marbella-to-san-pedro-de-alcantara-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walk from Marbella to San Pedro de Alcántara – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy coastal loop. Other longer loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén Viewpoint – Monda loop from Playa de Levante&lt;/a&gt;, offer more challenging circular options.

Q: Is parking available at popular road cycling trailheads in Marbella?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Marbella, as well as in the white villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Istán, Ojén, and Benahavís. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak seasons.

Wish I have discovered it earlier
A mountain harbor situated or 551 m s.n.m. From here we can descend back to Ojén and Marbella or climb up to the Refugio de Juanar (830 m)

This viewpoint offers beautiful views of one of the white villages of Andalusia - Ojén. Ojén is a town of 4,000 inhabitants surrounded by orchards. Its name comes from the Iberian root oixan, oihan or ojen which means forest.

faqs.question: Is the Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This climb is generally considered a moderately challenging experience, ideal for intermediate-level cyclists. While rewarding, it involves significant inclines and is demanding, so beginners might find it tough.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour starts from Marbella. You can find parking in Marbella, for example near the Marina de Marbella. Alternatively, you could start directly from Ojén, which would shorten the initial, busier section of the climb.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road surface like on the climb to Refugio Juanar?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies. The initial part, particularly on roads like the A-355, can be busy but has a nice and wide road surface. After passing through Ojén town and turning onto the MA-5300 towards Refugio Juanar, the road becomes smoother, prettier, and quieter, featuring beautiful switchbacks in the final 5 kilometers.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse landscapes, from Mediterranean pine forests and ravines of the Sierra Blanca to vineyards and olive groves. As you climb, the flora transitions to pine and oak forests. The route offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Costa del Sol and glimpses of Ojén, one of Andalusia's white villages. Don't miss the Ojén Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What are the key points of interest or landmarks on this climb?
faqs.answer: Notable points include the Ojén Pass Climb and the Ojén Pass itself. The Ojén Viewpoint offers expansive vistas. The destination, Refugio Juanar, is a mountain hotel providing refreshments and a peaceful atmosphere.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Ojén - Refugio Juanar Climb?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit for pleasant cycling weather, avoiding the intense heat of summer. The higher elevations offer shade and cooler temperatures, but it's always wise to check the forecast.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Sierra Blanca Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for road cycling this route through the Sierra Blanca Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and the natural environment when passing through protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural park itself may have provisions for dogs, this specific route is a road cycling climb. Road cycling with dogs, especially on routes that include busier sections and significant climbs, is generally not recommended for safety reasons.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the [CIMA ML01] Ojén - Refugio Juanar, Marbella - Ojén, and Tres Picos routes, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the end of the climb?
faqs.answer: Yes, Refugio Juanar, the destination of the climb, is a mountain hotel that offers accommodation, catering, and a bar. It's an ideal spot for refreshment after your ride and a base for further exploration.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for making the most of this climb?
faqs.answer: For a quieter and more scenic experience, focus on the section after Ojén town, particularly the MA-5300 towards Refugio Juanar. This last 5-kilometer stretch is praised for its smooth road, tranquility, and beautiful switchbacks, offering the most rewarding part of the climb.
